2012-14}} |ru_coachyearAend = |source = {{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/gary-mercer/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/coaches/gary-mercer/summary.html|title=Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}{{Rugby league records |id=1782 |title=Gary Mercer |access-date=19 July 2025}} }} '''Gary Ivan Mercer''' (born 22 June 1966) is a New Zealand [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] coach and former professional rugby league footballer. A [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand international representative]] player, he has spent most of his career in Britain both playing and coaching.{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/alltime.php|title=Castleford RLFC A to Z Player List (All Time)|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk |date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084010/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/alltime.php|archive-date=16 February 2012}}{{cite web|url=http://leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk/leedsrhinos/pastplayers/squad-page.asp?ID=161|title=Profile at leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk|publisher=leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081117100159/http://leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk/leedsrhinos/pastplayers/squad-page.asp?ID=100|archive-date=17 November 2008}} He is a former defence coach of [[Glasgow Warriors]] and a former coach at the SRU working in their [[Scottish Rugby Academy]].{{cite web|url=https://uk.linkedin.com/pub/gary-mercer/82/751/61b|title=Profile at uk.linkedin.com|publisher=uk.linkedin.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}} As of March 2016, he was the head coach of [[Biggar RFC]]. ==Playing career== In his long career he has represented the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] within rugby league and [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] sides and has played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]], [[Warrington Wolves]], [[Castleford Tigers]] and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]].{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1257218.stm|title=Mercer willing to play on|publisher=news.bbc.co.uk|date=3 April 2001|access-date=1 January 2002}} ===Challenge Cup Final appearances=== Gary Mercer played at {{rlp|ce}} in [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s 14-36 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1990 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1989–90 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 28 April 1990, in front of a crowd of 77,729.{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=455|title=1989-1990 Challenge Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2016|access-date=1 January 2017|archive-date=10 May 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170510181353/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=455|url-status=dead}} ===County Cup Final appearances=== Gary Mercer played {{rlp|fb}} in [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]]'s 12-12 draw with [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[1987 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1987–88 Rugby Football League season|1987–88 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 17 October 1987, and played at {{rlp|ce}} in the 11-2 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[1987 Yorkshire Cup]] Final [[Replay (sports)|replay]] during the [[1987–88 Rugby Football League season|1987–88 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 31 October 1987. ===Regal Trophy Final appearances=== Gary Mercer played at {{rlp|sr}} in [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s 12-2 victory over [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] in the [[1990–91 League Cup (rugby league)|1990–91 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 12 January 1991. ==Coaching career== ===Rugby League=== Mercer was also the [[player-coach]] at the [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax Blue Sox]], and coach at the [[Castleford Tigers]], his first game in charge was on 25 April 2004, and his last game in charge was on 18 September 2004.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2004/may/20/rugbyleague.castleford|title=Hanley in surprise return with Castleford|work=The Guardian|date=20 May 2004|access-date=1 January 2005}}{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/1938302.stm|title=Mercer coaxed out of retirement|publisher=news.bbc.co.uk|date=18 April 2002|access-date=1 January 2003}}{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk:80/coachprofile.php?p=coach&id=3|title=Coach Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk |date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070703050419/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/coachprofile.php?p=coach&id=3|archive-date=3 July 2007}} ===Rugby Union=== ====Glasgow Warriors==== Mercer switched to rugby union and became the defence coach for the [[Glasgow Warriors]] from June 2005 to June 2012{{cite web|url=https://www.scottishrugbyblog.co.uk/2012/03/the-shock-of-the-swap/|work=scottishrugbyblog.co.uk|title=The Shock of the Swap|date=1 March 2017|access-date=1 January 2013}} leaving the Warriors at the same time as [[Sean Lineen]]. While he was there praise was heaped on his defensive coaching.{{cite web|url=http://scottishrugby.org/news/07/11/07/exclusive-gary-mercer|work=scottishrugby.org|title=Exclusive: Gary Mercer|date=7 November 2007|access-date=1 January 2008|archive-date=10 May 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180510185026/http://www.scottishrugby.org/news/07/11/07/exclusive-gary-mercer|url-status=dead}} ====Scotland==== He became the Scotland Under-20 assistant coach and worked with the Under-21 side.{{cite web|url=https://www.glasgowwarriors.com/articles/coach/002961.php|title=Glasgow Warriors - Gary Mercer|publisher=glasgowwarriors.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}} He then worked for the [[Scottish Rugby Academy]].{{cite web|url=http://scottishrugby.org/news/14/08/22/mercer-depart-scottish-rugby|title=Mercer to depart Scottish Rugby|publisher=scottishrugby.org|date=22 August 2014|access-date=1 January 2015}} ====Yorkshire Carnegie==== He left the Scottish academy in August 2014{{cite news|url=http://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/yorkshire-carnegie-appoint-gary-mercer-as-head-coach-1-6800386|title=Yorkshire Carnegie appoint Gary Mercer as head coach|newspaper=Yorkshire Post|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}} to have a brief spell running [[Yorkshire Carnegie]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/31134428|title=Yorkshire Carnegie: Gary Mercer leaves Championship club|publisher=BBC|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}} ====Glasgow Hawks==== Mercer was the head coach of [[Glasgow Hawks]].{{cite web|url=http://www.glasgowhawks.com/teams/118813/coach/gary-mercer-1277135|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924022417/http://www.glasgowhawks.com/teams/118813/coach/gary-mercer-1277135|url-status=dead|archive-date=24 September 2015|title=Gary Mercer - 1st XV - Glasgow Hawks RFC|publisher=glasgowhawks.com|date=24 September 2015|access-date=1 January 2016}} ====Biggar==== Mercer moved on to Biggar.{{cite web|url=https://www.glasgowhawks.com/news/mercer-moves-on-1587493.html?|title=Mercer Moves On|publisher=glasgowhawks.com|date=18 April 2002|access-date=1 January 2003}} ====FUDs==== Founding member of the FUDs society, with co-members Toby Harris and Nathan Rainbow. ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links==
